title: Decisions, Pattern Matching, and Iteration · Program Defense
summary: Route support requests through priority rules, repeated records, and changed data conditions. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a support-request routing engine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Predict truth testing, chained comparisons, equality, identity, membership, and Boolean operator results.
Use if, elif, else, conditional expressions, and guard clauses to express exclusive and early-exit decisions.
Use match and case for structural patterns, captures, guards, alternatives, and exhaustive fallback behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
